2024-11-24.abstract: Operation Car Wash, which started in Brazil, revealed a bribery scheme that spanned at least 12 countries. Brazil’s Supreme Court has reversed much of its impact.

2024-11-24.title: What Two Acclaimed Female-Directed Movies Say About Indian Cinema
2024-11-24.abstract: “Laapataa Ladies” and “All We Imagine as Light” have both captivated audiences at home. But only one was able to get India’s nod for the best foreign film Oscar.

2024-11-24.title: An Israeli Rabbi Is Abducted and Killed in the U.A.E.
2024-11-24.abstract: The Israeli authorities called the killing of Rabbi Zvi Kogan an act of terrorism. The Emirati government said it had arrested three people in connection with the murder.

2024-11-24.title: In West Bank Raids, Palestinians See Echoes of Israel’s Gaza War
2024-11-24.abstract: West Bank residents say Israeli forces are adopting tactics similar to the ones they are deploying in Gaza, including airstrikes and the use of Palestinians as human shields.

2024-11-25.title: Uruguay’s Center Left Reclaims Power in Presidential Election
2024-11-25.abstract: After a campaign notable for the amiable tone from both sides, Yamandú Orsi was named the winner.

2024-11-25.title: DHL Cargo Plane Crashes Near Airport in Lithuania, Killing 1
2024-11-25.abstract: The crash near Vilnius Airport sparked a fire in a residential area, officials said. One person on board was killed and three others were hospitalized.

2024-11-25.title: Ultranationalist Leads First Round of Romania’s Presidential Election
2024-11-25.abstract: Calin Georgescu, who had been widely dismissed as a fringe candidate, won the most votes on Sunday with 22.9 percent. He will face a more centrist opponent in the second round on Dec. 8.

2024-11-25.title: Philippines President Slams Vice President’s Assassination Plot
2024-11-25.abstract: Political turmoil has escalated after Vice President Sara Duterte claimed to have arranged for President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.’s assassination if she is slain.

2024-11-25.title: On the Outskirts of Beirut, a Crowd Watches the War, and Waits for Its End
2024-11-25.abstract: Every night, dozens gather at the hillside to watch airstrikes rain down on the city’s southern suburbs. The ritual offers a window into the war — and proof that the once unimaginable is really happening.

2024-11-26.title: Israeli Strikes Threaten Lebanon’s Archaeological Treasures
2024-11-26.abstract: The country is home to thousands of years’ worth of antiquities. Some have already been damaged or destroyed in the war, alarming the conservationists trying to protect them.

2024-11-26.title: As Russia Advances in Ukraine, a Cop Has to Flee City After City
2024-11-26.abstract: For Volodymyr Nikulin, being a wartime police officer means aiding evacuees, surviving a shrapnel wound and tackling looters. It also means quickly switching cities when one falls to the invaders.

2024-11-26.title: A Misstep by Ireland’s Prime Minister Muddies Election Race
2024-11-26.abstract: A fumbled meeting with a voter may dent Simon Harris’s popularity. But his coalition government’s main opposition, the nationalist party Sinn Fein, will likely remain locked out of power.

2024-11-26.title: Rescuers Walk 14 Hours to Save Sole Survivor of Costa Rica Plane Crash
2024-11-26.abstract: Rescue workers trekked overnight across a remote mountain forest carrying a woman who had survived a crash that killed five of the six people aboard.

2024-11-26.title: Rocket Engine Fire Stalls Japan’s Ambitions to Launch Satellites
2024-11-26.abstract: Japan is off to a shaky start in its efforts to break into the space rocket market dominated by Elon Musk’s SpaceX.

2024-11-27.title: Meet One of Hong Kong’s Last Remaining Pro-Democracy Activists
2024-11-27.abstract: For Chan Po-ying, a labor rights leader, life is one of constant police surveillance, even on hikes. But she finds solace from tiny gestures of support.

2024-11-27.title: Will Namibia’s Liberation Party Be the Next to Fall in Africa?
2024-11-27.abstract: Voters head to the polls on Wednesday in the latest test for one of southern Africa’s liberation parties, which have been rebuked at the ballot box this year.

2024-11-27.title: ‘Mr. Every Man’: The 50 Others Accused in France’s Mass Rape Trial
2024-11-27.abstract: Dominique Pelicot invited men to rape his wife, whom he had drugged. The French media call them “Mr. Every Man” because they come from such ordinary backgrounds.

2024-11-27.title: Britain’s Emotional Debate Over Assisted Dying
2024-11-27.abstract: As lawmakers prepare to vote on legalizing assisted suicide for the terminally ill in England and Wales, an intense public discussion has unfolded.

2024-11-27.title: Record November Snowfall Hits Seoul
2024-11-27.abstract: The first snowfall of the season grounded flights and triggered a weather warning throughout the South Korean capital.

2024-11-28.title: E.U. Vessels Surround Anchored Chinese Ship After Baltic Sea Cables Are Severed
2024-11-28.abstract: Multiple countries are investigating and the authorities in Europe say they have not ruled out sabotage. But U.S. intelligence officials have assessed that the cables were not cut deliberately.

2024-11-28.title: What the Cease-Fire Tells Us About Hezbollah
2024-11-28.abstract: Hezbollah has entered a cease-fire with Israel. Ben Hubbard, an international correspondent for The New York Times, explains how 13 months of war left Hezbollah weakened, isolated and desperate for a way to stop the fighting in Lebanon.

2024-11-28.title: Syrian Rebel Groups Launch Largest Offensive in Years
2024-11-28.abstract: Scores of people were killed after forces opposed to the government of President Bashar al-Assad overtook a Syrian military base, a monitoring group reported.

2024-11-28.title: London’s 850-Year-Old Smithfield Meat Market Is Set to Close
2024-11-28.abstract: The historic site in Britain’s capital has been a market for centuries. The local authority voted this week to close it.

2024-11-28.title: These Exams Mean Everything in India. Thieves See a Gold Mine.
2024-11-28.abstract: In a country where government jobs are highly coveted, the tests that govern hiring are a lucrative target for criminal gangs.

2024-11-29.title: Canada Accuses Google of Creating an Ad Tech Monopoly
2024-11-29.abstract: The case largely echoes an antitrust action in the United States and seeks to force Google to sell off sections of its online ad business.

2024-11-29.title: Why Mexico Is Eliminating Independent Watchdog Agencies
2024-11-29.abstract: A vote in the country’s Senate has cleared the way to abolish seven independent organizations that provided oversight on issues such as public information and price fixing.

2024-11-29.title: A Dublin Neighborhood at the Sharp End of Ireland’s Election Issues
2024-11-29.abstract: In a northeast neighborhood of the capital city, deprivation and affluence exist side-by-side, and many voters feel frustrated by the status quo.

2024-11-29.title: Chinese Court Sentences Journalist to 7 Years in Prison for Espionage
2024-11-29.abstract: Dong Yuyu, who has written articles critical of the Communist Party, was arrested in 2022 while having lunch with a Japanese diplomat.

2024-11-29.title: Ukraine’s River of Anguish
2024-11-29.abstract: Amphibious operations across the Dnipro River at Kherson illustrated the suffering and endurance of Ukrainian forces. A team of Times journalists observed the perilous movements.

2024-11-30.title: Taiwan’s President to Rally Tiny Pacific Allies to Counter China
2024-11-30.abstract: Lai Ching-te is looking to shore up support from three island nations that are among a dwindling number to keep diplomatic ties with Taiwan.

2024-11-30.title: Undersea Surgeons
2024-11-30.abstract: The cables at the heart of the lightspeed, globe-spanning internet run across the grimy, perilous, inaccessible deeps of the sea, in places no one ever sees or visits – until the cables break.

2024-11-30.title: What Happens When Undersea Internet Cables Snap?
2024-11-30.abstract: The internet is made up of hundreds of cables crossing the floors and the canyons of the earth’s oceans. So what happens when the cables snap? James Glanz, an investigative reporter for The New York Times, explains what could go wrong with subsea internet cables and how these cables get fixed.

2024-11-30.title: How Kennedy Has Worked Abroad to Weaken Global Public Health Policy
2024-11-30.abstract: The health secretary pick and his organization have worked around the world to undermine longstanding policies on measles, AIDS and more.

2024-11-30.title: Israel-Hezbollah Cease-Fire Rests on a Wobbly Linchpin: Lebanon’s Army
2024-11-30.abstract: The Lebanese Army is tasked with ensuring that Hezbollah abides by the cease-fire. It has failed at that task before.
